overview: This venomous snake is consistent with a Cottonmouth (Agkistrodon piscivorus), a pit viper native to the southeastern United States. Wilmington Star-News +1 It shares characteristics with other vipers, such as a triangular head shape and heat-sensing pits located between the eye and nostril. Reddit +1 These snakes are generally not aggressive and primarily avoid human interaction, preferring to flee or display defensive behaviors if cornered. Wilmington Star-News
